summary: Iteration 3 testing: pairing realism (short-haul out-and-back), IncidentQueue default ALL filter fix, advisor send-btn no-occlusion fix, and full UI E2E. Backend 13/13 pytest passed (10 legacy + 3 new pairing tests). Frontend E2E walked through boot -> roster -> assign with pairing notice -> start-day -> play (clock advanced) -> tick -> resolve incident (status RESOLVED visible) -> advisor with real Claude Sonnet 4.5 response -> end-day -> debrief. All critical assertions satisfied.

issues: Column shows sequence + route ('1/2 · LHR-MXP → MXP-LHR') rather than the literal text 'OUT-BACK' mentioned in the requirements. Semantically equivalent (clearly conveys out-and-back pairing) so not a blocker; flagging for awareness if literal label is desired.

action_items: Optional: if literal 'OUT-BACK' string is desired in roster PAIRING column, prepend it to the existing display.
critical_code_review_comments: simulation.py FDP rule correctly sums whole-pairing block + per-sector turnaround; long-haul threshold 360min appropriately picks MAX_FDP_MIN_LONGHAUL.
server.py advisor route correctly returns graceful fallback with status 200 when LLM fails (UI never breaks).
AssignModal pairing-notice covers both branches (multi-sector short-haul and long-haul block>360); good DRY.
IncidentQueue default filter is now 'ALL' - resolved cards remain visible with data-testid='incident-status-{id}' === 'RESOLVED'.
AdvisorPanel form has mb-12 to clear Emergent badge - elementFromPoint at send-btn center returns the button itself (no overlay).
seed_data_creation: Each test creates a fresh game via POST /api/sim/new
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/ - run with: pytest backend/tests/ -v. Pairing logic: A320 sectors come in pairs sharing pairing_id; long-haul sectors are solo. assign_crew/unassign_crew propagate across pairing_id siblings; response includes pairing_sectors. Frontend E2E flow validated end-to-end on https://ce94728a-8b91-4632-959e-bccf13261590.preview.emergentagent.com .
rca of the issue: N/A - no regressions found. All three iteration-3 deliverables verified: (1) Pairings realism: A320 flights generated as out-and-back pairs sharing pairing_id; assign returns pairing_sectors=2 and crew_id appears in BOTH sectors; unassign on either sector removes from BOTH. Long-haul flights are solo with their own pairing_id. (2) IncidentQueue default filter changed to ALL - resolved incidents remain visible with RESOLVED status badge. (3) Advisor send-btn unobstructed (elementFromPoint at center returns the button itself, not the Emergent badge) and Claude Sonnet 4.5 returns a real contextual response naming specific flights.
